title: Support escalation waiting on customer response
summary: Drives a customer support ticket through triage, outbound contact, a blocking waiting state pending the customer's reply, and final resolution. Demonstrates the kernel's blocking-obligation pattern: the workflow sits in `status=waiting` until the obligation is satisfied with `customer_response` evidence.

task.goal: Customer cannot complete checkout when paying via stored card on file.

steps.summary: Supervisor creates the workflow task pinned to support_escalation@1 and binds the support agent.

args.goal: Customer cannot complete checkout when paying via stored card on file.

steps.summary: Support agent attaches their triage summary.

evidence.summary: Failure surfaces only with stored cards; raw card flow works. Suspect tokenization edge case.

steps.summary: Support agent records the customer outreach (email asking for the failing card token reference).

evidence.summary: Asked customer for masked card token + timestamps of last 3 attempts.

steps.summary: Apply transition `wait_for_customer`: active/contact_customer -> waiting/awaiting_customer. Effects: create blocking obligation `customer_response_pending` (owner=customer_proxy), start 72h timer.

Customer replies. Supervisor satisfies the blocking obligation by attaching `customer_response` evidence. Note: the kernel does not auto-bind `customer_proxy` during `satisfy_obligation`; the role stays unbound for this scenario. The role exists primarily as the obligation's `ownerRole` declaration. To exercise `autoBindOnFirstRun`, a transition `by: ['customer_proxy']` would be required (not part of the happy path here).

evidence.summary: Customer supplied masked card token tok_***4242 and timestamps.

expectedStateAfter.note: status remains waiting until resume_resolution is applied; obligation is now satisfied.

steps.summary: Support agent attaches the resolution note and closes the ticket as resolved.

evidence.summary: Reset stored token; reissued via processor; customer confirmed checkout works.

alternateBranches.name: no-reply-abandon
alternateBranches.summary: If customer never replies and the 72h timer elapses, supervisor applies `abandon_no_reply` (supervisorBypass) -> closed/abandoned.

alternateBranches.name: escalate-to-engineering
alternateBranches.summary: After step 09 the support agent may apply `escalate_to_engineering` instead of `resolve`, attaching `escalation_packet`. This emits a `create_child_task` effect targeting `hotfix_fastlane@1`.

negativeChecks.name: resume-without-obligation
negativeChecks.summary: Skip step 07 and try `resume_resolution`: the kernel rejects with `obligation_not_satisfied` (the open blocking `customer_response_pending` obligation has not been satisfied). The transition's evidence requirement also fails for `customer_response`, but the obligation check fires first.

negativeChecks.name: wait-prevents-direct-resolve
negativeChecks.summary: From `waiting/awaiting_customer` attempt `resolve` directly. `resolve.from = active/resolution`, so the kernel rejects with `state_mismatch` before evaluating any other requirement.
